presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: Severe chest pain radiating to my back
Worst chest pain of my life
Sudden chest and upper back pain
Sharp pain that moved into my back

history_data.hpi: Patient experienced sudden onset severe chest pain approximately 45 minutes ago while resting. The pain was maximal at onset, described as tearing, and radiates between the shoulder blades. Associated with sweating and severe anxiety.
patient_quotes: It felt like something ripped inside my chest.
The pain shot straight through to my back.
This is the worst pain I've ever felt.
I honestly thought I was dying.

better_with: nothing — pain is constant and severe

cardiovascular: Blood pressure difference between arms (>20 mmHg)

investigations.ECG: Sinus tachycardia. Left ventricular hypertrophy. Non-specific ST-T changes. No STEMI pattern.
investigations.Troponin: 0.06 ng/mL (mildly elevated — myocardial ischaemia secondary to coronary ostial involvement)
investigations.Chest X-Ray: Widened mediastinum. Enlarged aortic silhouette. Abnormal aortic contour. No pneumothorax.
investigations.CT Aortogram: Stanford Type A aortic dissection. Intimal flap identified in ascending aorta extending to descending thoracic aorta. No pericardial tamponade.
investigations.D-Dimer: 3.8 mg/L FEU (markedly elevated — supports aortic dissection; PE also possible but clinical picture favours dissection)
investigations.FBC (Full Blood Count): WBC 13.6 × 10⁹/L (leukocytosis — stress response), Hb 12.9 g/dL, Platelets 198 × 10⁹/L
investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 140 mmol/L, K 4.2 mmol/L, Creatinine 104 µmol/L — mildly elevated, possible renal artery involvement
investigations.Blood Group & Crossmatch: Group O positive. 4 units packed red cells crossmatched and held.
investigations.Echocardiogram: Aortic root dilatation. Moderate aortic regurgitation. Hyperdynamic LV function. No pericardial effusion.

hidden_diagnosis: Acute Stanford Type A Aortic Dissection

critical_actions: measure blood pressure in both arms
management_actions: Heart rate and blood pressure control (target SBP 100–120 mmHg)
